title: Multi-column IN clause for subquery produces wrong results
poc: CREATE TABLE T1 (C1 INT, C2 INT, C3 INT);
SELECT C1, C2, COUNT(*) FROM T1 GROUP BY C1, C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT T1.C1, T1.C2, T1.C3 FROM T1, (SELECT C1, C2 FROM T1 GROUP BY C1, C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) X WHERE T1.C1 = X.C1 AND T1.C2 = X.C2;
SELECT C1, C2, C3 FROM T1 WHERE (C1, C2) IN (SELECT C1, C2 FROM T1 GROUP BY C1, C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
SELECT C1, C2, C3 FROM T1 WHERE (C2, C1) IN (SELECT C2, C1 FROM T1 GROUP BY C1, C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
SELECT C1, C2, C3 FROM T1 WHERE (C1, C2) IN (SELECT T1.C1, T1.C2 FROM T1 GROUP BY T1.C1, T1.C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
SELECT C1, C2, C3 FROM T1 WHERE (C1, C2) IN (SELECT T.C1, T.C2 FROM T1 AS T GROUP BY T.C1, T.C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
SELECT C1, C2, C3 FROM T1 WHERE (C1, C2) NOT IN (SELECT C1, C2 FROM T1 GROUP BY C1, C2 HAVING COUNT(*) = 1);
key_bug_features: Multi-column IN clause with subquery produces incorrect results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE T2 (C1 INT NOT NULL, C2 VARCHAR(50), C3 FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0);
SELECT C1, C2 FROM T2 WHERE (C1, C2) IN (SELECT C1, C2 FROM T2 WHERE C3 > 2.0);
SELECT C1, C2 FROM T2 WHERE (C1, C2) NOT IN (SELECT C1, C2 FROM T2 WHERE C3 IS NULL);
SELECT C1, C2 FROM T2 WHERE (C1, C2) IN (SELECT T2.C1, T2.C2 FROM T2 GROUP BY T2.C1, T2.C2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
